question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: parse(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that parserContext.clear() is called in the finally block even on successful parse, and if the @Transactional commit happens after the finally block clears the context, cause a race condition where another thread reads an empty parserContext before the commit, leading to incomplete transaction data being gossiped?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: doParse()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a RecordFile where recordFile.getSidecars() returns a collection containing null elements, causing parserContext.addAll() to throw a NullPointerException after all transactions have been processed via recordItemListener.onItem(), rolling back the @Transactional and preventing all transactions in the file from being gossiped?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: updateIndex()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a sequence of RecordFiles where a v5 file is followed by a v6 file with an index that is exactly 1 greater than the v5 file's index, causing the offset to be zero and updateIndex() to skip the repository.updateIndex() call, leaving all historical v5 block numbers with incorrect indices that prevent consumers from finding those transactions by block number?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: doParse()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a RecordFile where the dateRangeCalculator.getFilter() call returns a cached DateRangeFilter from a previous parse cycle with an endDate already in the past, causing all items in the new file to be filtered out by dateRangeFilter.filter() and never passed to recordItemListener.onItem(), silently suppressing all transactions in the file?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: parse(List<RecordFile>)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a List<RecordFile> where the first file is valid but the second file causes a HashMismatchException, and because the @Transactional wraps the entire list parse, the first file's transactions (already processed by recordItemListener.onItem()) are rolled back, permanently preventing those transactions from being gossiped?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: doParse()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a RecordFile where aggregator.update(recordFile) throws an unchecked exception due to arithmetic overflow in gas accumulation (e.g., sum of gasUsed values exceeds Long.MAX_VALUE), aborting doParse() after all items have been processed via recordItemListener.onItem() but before parserContext.add(recordFile) is called, causing the @Transactional to roll back all transactions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: shouldParse() (AbstractStreamFileParser)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the getLast() method's fallback to streamFileRepository.findLatest() when the in-memory last AtomicReference is null, by poisoning the repository with a RecordFile that has a future consensusEnd, causing all subsequent RecordFiles to be skipped by shouldParse() and their transactions to be permanently suppressed?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: doParse()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a RecordFile where recordFile.getItems() returns items in non-chronological order, causing the logIndex AtomicInteger to assign incorrect indices to transactions, making them appear out-of-order in the database and preventing consumers from correctly reconstructing the gossip sequence?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: parse(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @Retryable exponential backoff (minBackoff, maxBackoff, multiplier from recordParserProperties) by causing repeated retryable failures, creating a situation where the retry delay grows so large that transactions are effectively delayed indefinitely, preventing timely gossip of those transactions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: doParse()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a RecordFile where parserContext.add(recordFile) is called with a RecordFile object that has already been cleared (bytes set to null by a prior clear() call), causing the persisted RecordFile in the database to have null bytes, making it impossible for downstream consumers to re-read and re-gossip the transactions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: doParse()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a RecordFile where the RecordFileParsedEvent is published at line 138 before any items are processed, and if an exception occurs during item processing causing a @Transactional rollback, the event has already been published with the consensusEnd, causing downstream listeners to believe those transactions were gossiped when they were actually rolled back?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: parse(List<RecordFile>)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a List<RecordFile> containing duplicate RecordFiles (same consensusStart/consensusEnd), causing the second duplicate to be skipped by shouldParse() due to the 'previous.getConsensusEnd() >= current.getConsensusStart()' check, but the first copy's transactions to be committed, creating an inconsistency where only half the expected transactions are gossiped?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: doParse()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a RecordItem where getConsensusTimestamp() returns 0 (epoch), causing dateRangeFilter.filter(0) to return false if the filter's start is STARTUP_TIME (which is always > 0), silently dropping that transaction from recordItemListener.onItem() while still including it in aggregator.accept(), creating a ghost transaction in block aggregates?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: doParse()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a RecordFile where recordItemListener.onItem() throws a non-retryable exception (e.g., a DataIntegrityViolationException not in the retryable list) for one specific RecordItem, causing the forEach loop to abort and the @Transactional to roll back, preventing all remaining items in the file from being gossiped even though they are individually valid?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: shouldParse() (AbstractStreamFileParser)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the condition 'if (previous == null) { return true; }' in shouldParse() by ensuring the streamFileRepository.findLatest() returns empty (e.g., by timing the attack during initial startup), causing a RecordFile with a forged previousHash to be accepted without hash chain verification, allowing injection of fabricated transactions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: doParse()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a RecordFile where the items iterable is backed by a concurrent collection that is modified during iteration, causing a ConcurrentModificationException mid-forEach, aborting processing after some items have been sent to recordItemListener.onItem() and rolling back the @Transactional, preventing all items from being gossiped?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: parse(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the interaction between @Retryable and @Transactional where each retry creates a new transaction, and if the database has a unique constraint on consensusTimestamp, the second retry attempt fails with a constraint violation (not retryable) after the first retry partially committed some items, causing permanent loss of the remaining transactions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: doParse()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a RecordFile where recordFile.setCount(count.get()) is called with a count that is lower than the actual number of items (because some were filtered by dateRangeFilter), causing the stored RecordFile to report an incorrect transaction count that misleads consumers into thinking fewer transactions were gossiped than actually exist in the block?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: updateIndex()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a v6 RecordFile with a negative index value, causing the offset calculation (recordFile.getIndex() - lastRecordFile.getIndex() - 1) to produce a large positive number, triggering repository.updateIndex() with a massive offset that corrupts all block indices in the database and makes all historical transactions unreachable?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: doParse()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a RecordFile where the dateRangeCalculator.getFilter() is called inside doParse() on every invocation but returns a cached filter (ConcurrentHashMap in DateRangeCalculator), and by racing with a filter cache clear operation, cause doParse() to use a stale filter that excludes all current transactions from being gossiped?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: parse(List<RecordFile>)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a List<RecordFile> where the last file in the list is the only one that passes shouldParse(), causing doFlush() to be called only on that last file, but because the @Transactional wraps the entire list, a failure in the last file's flush causes all previously processed transactions (from earlier files in the list) to be rolled back?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: doParse()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a RecordFile where recordFile.getItems() returns an infinite or very long stream, causing the forEach loop to run indefinitely, holding the synchronized lock on parse() and blocking all other transaction gossip indefinitely, constituting a denial-of-service against the gossip pipeline?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: doParse()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a RecordItem where getTransaction().getSerializedSize() throws an exception (e.g., due to a malformed protobuf), causing recordMetrics() to throw inside the forEach loop and abort processing of all subsequent items, preventing those transactions from being gossiped?
